title: Aflatoxin Contamination of Groundnut: Conventional Breeding for Resistance
abstract:
Aflatoxin contamination of groundnut or peanut (Arachis hypogaea L.) is a widespread serious
problem in most groundnut-producing countries where the crop is grown under rainfed conditions. The
semi-arid tropical environment is conducive to preharvest contamination when the crop experiences
drought before harvest, whereas in the wet and humid areas, postharvest contamination is more
prevalent. Breeding
